pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the release flag for Java 9+ compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer JDKs; using release ensures consistent compilation for the target version.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add lombok-mapstruct-binding to the annotation processor paths.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this, MapStruct may fail to process Lombok-generated getters/setters correctly during compilation.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Migrate Spring Security HTTP configuration from the deprecated lambda-less DSL to the new lambda-based DSL required by Spring Security 6.x.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The methods like authorizeHttpRequests(), csrf(), logout(), and formLogin() without arguments are removed in Spring Security 6; using them causes a compilation error.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Update the application of the custom authorization server configurer to use the new .with() method and withDefaults() helper.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The .apply() method on HttpSecurity is removed in Spring Security 6; using it causes a compilation error.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add the missing static import for withDefaults() required by the new configurer application syntax.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The symbol withDefaults is not found without the import, causing a compilation error.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Migrate HttpSecurity configuration from the deprecated fluent API (using .and()) to the new lambda-based DSL required by Spring Security 6.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The .and() method and the old fluent chaining style for HttpSecurity configuration were removed in Spring Security 6, causing compilation errors if not updated to the lambda-based syntax.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ preview and Java 17+ standard requirements for serializable fields.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Compilation error: 'serialVersionUID' is not a serializable field (or similar warning treated as error) because the @Serial annotation is required for fields related to serialization in modern Java versions.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Compilation error: cannot find symbol class Serial.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 strict serialization checks.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Java 21 enables strong encapsulation and stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for proper serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compilation error.
